name: Servo
description: An autonomous local-AI executive layer. One persistent process that perceives, reasons, and acts on its own environment through a role-based overlay system, organized into three canonical layers: Cortex (ephemeral runtime), Persona (identity + overlays), Codex (on-disk truth).

layers.cortex: The six-step core loop (core/loop.py). Ephemeral runtime; allowed to lose state on restart. Reference implementation, expected to be obsoleted by future models with embedded schedulers.
layers.persona: A single identity (Servo) expressed through role overlays. Invariant identity lives in codex/persona_core.md and is injected into the system prompt as the [IDENTITY] block on every cycle. Role overlays (roles.json) supply voice/format/risk fields under [ACTIVE ROLE]. Permanent, portable, backend-agnostic.
layers.codex: The canonical on-disk truth — persona_core, manifest, skill_map, role_overlays, role_manifests, lexicon, decisions, glossary, history, rejected_proposals. Role-writable: any role may append to decisions and history; Orchestrator proposes updates to skill_map and role_manifests/ via change proposals. persona_core is hand-edited by Kevin only. Architecture reviews are model-scoped and live under workspace/<model>/ (see workspace_policy).

steps.name: PERCEIVE
steps.summary: Classify the current input (user message, chained tool payload, goal-due prod, system event).

steps.name: CONTEXTUALIZE
steps.summary: Load conversation_history turns from SQLite, relevant episodic memory from Chroma, available tools, active goals, working memory.

steps.name: REASON
steps.summary: Call the model with system prompt + messages. Handle truncation via auto-continue (bounded by max_auto_continues).

steps.name: ACT
steps.summary: Execute a tool if one was requested, feed the result back for a follow-up response, handle truncation again.

steps.name: INTEGRATE
steps.summary: Persist turns and tool outcomes, update working memory, emit telemetry, decide the next directive.

steps.name: OBSERVE
steps.summary: Passive watch for external triggers (time-based goals, user input, file changes) while doing minimal work, then GOTO 1.

roles.summary: One personality (Servo) expressed through role-specific voice overlays. When no role is active, the agent is Servo with no overlay applied — this is represented by the explicit 'servo' entry in roles.json.
roles.identity_injection: codex/persona_core.md is loaded (mtime-cached) and inserted as [IDENTITY]. The active role's overlay fields are rendered as [ACTIVE ROLE]. When no role is active, the servo entry is used so the identity + overlay blocks are always present.
roles.persona_update_mechanism: TODO — no automated curator yet. codex/persona_core.md is hand-edited by Kevin.

stack.llm: Ollama HTTP (http://localhost:11434) — controls num_predict and conversation_history only; num_ctx is not touched
stack.state: SQLite (WAL) for conversation/memory/trace/state; ChromaDB for episodic vector memory (auto-pruned to 1000 entries)
stack.logging: Sentinel JSONL with rotation (5 MB threshold)
stack.tools: Dynamic registry via importlib. Contract: TOOL_NAME, TOOL_DESCRIPTION, TOOL_ENABLED, TOOL_SCHEMA, execute(...)

hardware_policy.critical_gate: Critical requires BOTH RAM >= 95% AND VRAM >= 95% (AND-gate in core/hardware.py).
hardware_policy.throttle_action: Clear KV cache, reduce conversation_history by 2 with a model-appropriate floor (default_conversation_history // 2).
hardware_policy.restore_action: After 5 consecutive stable cycles, restore conversation_history to the model default.

loop_control.chain_limit: Max chained tool calls within one user turn (when continuous_mode is off).
loop_control.autonomous_loop_limit: Max continuous-mode re-loops before pausing for user review. 0 = unbounded.
loop_control.max_consecutive_grace: Max consecutive no-progress grace cycles before forcing 'done' in continuous mode.

interruption.policy: submit_input sets a cancel_event that interrupts an in-flight Ollama request mid-stream. The cycle catches ChatCancelled and re-enters PERCEIVE with the new user input. Partial responses are discarded.

role_selection.policy: When multiple role_* goals are simultaneously due, pick by (priority asc, most-overdue desc). priority is a 1-5 field on each role in roles.json; default 5 if missing.

workspace_policy.model_workspace: workspace/<model_safe_name>/ — per-model scratch (architecture_review_<v>.md, change_proposal_<v>.md, critique_<v>.md, calibration, screenshots, old_stuff). Scholar emits a new architecture_review_<v>.md each cycle and bumps the prior version to old_stuff/. Architect emits change_proposal_<v>.md; Analyst emits matching critique_<v>.md.
workspace_policy.codex_path: codex/ — canonical truth (persona_core, manifest, manifest_compact, skill_map, role_overlays, role_manifests/, lexicon, decisions, glossary, history, rejected_proposals). Role-writable: any role may append to decisions and history; Orchestrator proposes updates to skill_map and role_manifests/ via change proposals. persona_core is hand-edited by Kevin only.
workspace_policy.sandbox: Filesystem access (read and write) is scoped to the project directory. Autonomous writes are permitted in workspace/<model>/ (default scratch) and codex/ (canonical maintenance).
workspace_policy.legacy_paths: Pre-0.4.0 <model>_notes/ folders have been consolidated under workspace/. Any remaining reference to *_notes/ is a migration bug.

codex_contents.role_overlays.md: Canonical reference for the overlay system.
codex_contents.role_manifests/: Per-role detailed manifests (sentinel, analyst, architect, orchestrator, scholar).
codex_contents.rejected_proposals.md: Change proposals that were considered and rejected, with rationale.
codex_contents.log_digest.md: Append-only, machine-written digest of cold log entries. Maintained by tools/log_summarizer.py — Phase 5 pilot. Never hand-edit.

memory_summarization.scope: cold log entries (age > 24h) + conversation history at INTEGRATE — kernel shared across all callers

destinations.conversation_history: SQLite conversation_summary table — surfaced to the model as the [PRIOR CONTEXT] block in the system prompt; raw turns covered by the summary are filtered out of the Ollama message list

triggers.cold_logs: Scholar invokes log_summarizer as a normal tool call on its 120-minute schedule
triggers.conversation_history: core/loop.py::_integrate calls history_compressor.maybe_compress() at end of each non-transient turn; fires when uncompressed turns >= 2 x conversation_history (scales with hardware throttle). Empty-response backoff: wait +conversation_history more turns before retry.

owners.conversation_history: Cortex loop (automatic, non-destructive, no user toggle)

memory_summarization.kernel: tools/summarizer.py exposes summarize(user_content, system_rules, *, model, timeout, max_input_chars) and detect_loaded_model(fallback). Owns Ollama client wiring, 300s chat timeout, 2s /api/ps probe, tail-preserve last-resort trim, empty-response passthrough. Does NOT own prompt content, input shaping, destination, or the agent tool contract. See decisions.md D-20260418-10 (Phase 1 extraction) and D-20260419-01 (Phase 2 conversation hook).

task_ledger.purpose: User-directed multi-step plan persistence. Lower-altitude replacement for the v0.7.0-purged goals system — no scheduler, no background firing, no role binding, just an explicit operator-approved sequence of milestones the model is expected to work through.
task_ledger.tool: tools/task.py — single tool, four actions: create (batch or single), complete (idempotent), list (renders cursor), clear (wipes ledger).
task_ledger.storage: SQLite tasks table (id, description, status, created_at, completed_at). Sticky across /reset — clear_conversation does NOT wipe tasks; only `task clear` or StateStore.clear_tasks() does.
task_ledger.grain: Semantic milestones, not tool calls. One task per real unit of progress. soft_max=20 entries per create-batch; over-cap is rejected with a teaching error rather than silently truncated.
task_ledger.prompt_injection: core/loop.py::_render_active_tasks_block produces an [ACTIVE TASKS] block. Arrow cursor on first pending row, [x] on completed rows. Empty ledger renders as the empty string. Spliced between [PATH DISCIPLINE] and AVAILABLE TOOLS so plan context sits adjacent to tool menu.

stuck_detection_nudge.branch: core/loop.py::_run_cycle — fires after chain-branch, before continuous-mode gate.
stuck_detection_nudge.trigger: Pending ledger non-empty AND no chained tool call AND inbound payload lacks _task_nudge marker.
stuck_detection_nudge.gating: UNGATED from continuous_mode — fires whenever there's pending plan work regardless of checkbox. Bounded by autonomous_loop_limit and the _task_nudge single-fire guard (prevents runaway self-loop).
stuck_detection_nudge.payload: Transient SYSTEM message naming cursor task id/description and remaining count. _transient: True, so never persisted to conversation history.

task_ledger.directive_dispatch: run() handles action="task_nudge" unconditionally, parallel to chain. Works in both continuous and single-turn modes.
